observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed with the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to:
Hire or assign personnel that will be solely in-charged of encoding the incoming ITRs to be submitted to PHIC and ensure the timely filing of claims; and
Support the renewal of accreditation of the Birthing Homes in barangays Baylimango and Barcelona by providing the necessary hiring of personnel and purchase of supplies and materials;
The CHO to prepare and submit to OCA, a monthly List of Reimbursable Claims filed to PHIC, and regularly monitor the claims submitted against the claims reimbursed, and file for necessary action to PHIC in case of deficiencies; and
The OCA to record revenue and Due from GOCC-PHIC in the GF books, upon receipt of the List of Reimbursable Claims filed to PHIC (in letter b) from CHO; and record the receipt of reimbursements simultaneously in the TF and GF books with the following suggested accounting entries:

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The City Accountant to:
Record the deficiency of leave benefits for unpaid officials and employees with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Leave Benefits Payable amounting P1.582 million, subject to appropriation and cash back-up duly certified by the City Budget Officer and Treasurer; and
Ensure that leave benefits earned and paid are recorded in the appropriate account-Terminal Leave Benefits (expense), moving forward.
The CHRMO to submit the List of Accumulated Leave Credits of Officials and Employee of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and every quarter thereof, to the LFC thru the Office of the City Budget for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds.
The LFC:
To prioritize the appropriation of earned leave credits of retired officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024 in the amount of ₱1.582 million; and
Upon receipt of the List of the accumulated earned leave credits as of December 31, 2024, and every quarter thereof, from the CHRMO, to consider appropriating funds for its purpose prioritizing the retiring employees and elected officials.

We recommended, and the City Accountant agreed to:
Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments and submit the reconciliation thereof to the Office of the Auditor; and
Require the personnel assigned in maintaining the ledgers of these accounts to have a monthly reconciliation report duly signed by all concern and shall form part of the regular report for submission to Office of the Auditor.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The LFC through the City Budget Officer and implementing offices to conduct thorough planning of expenditures specially to those recurring special activities of the city and properly classify its object of expenditures in the Annual Budget in consonance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs, to ensure proper recording of transactions in the books of accounts.
The City Accounting Office to:
Strengthen review of transactions to ensure proper classification and recording to its appropriate accounts;
Provide correct and complete disclosures of OMOE account in the Notes to FS; and
Moving forward, to avoid charging Water/Power Supply System, Repairs and Maintenance, Donations and Confidential Expenses to OMOE account.

We recommended, and Management through the City Accountant, agreed to:
Record in the box F (accounting entries) of the DVs the journal entries and the JEV number in recording the accounts payable so that upon payment of the said DVs, it can be traced that this transaction had previously been recorded as accounts payable and mark “AP” all supporting documents thereof to indicate that it has been recognized as payable;
Ensure the reliability of the Accounts Payable, copy of the DV, CAFOA and proof of service rendered or delivery of goods are attached to the JEV recognizing the liability; and
Request for a statement of account from the creditor as of December 31, 2024 and reconcile the same to its accounting records, and furnish the Auditor of the reconciliation statement, for the accounts affected by the adjustment made on January 31, 2025.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The LCE issue a memorandum creating the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee following the composition in the COA Circular and relieved them from their regular duties for three months; and
The Inventory Committee to:
Update the PIP for the completion of the one-time cleansing and furnish to COA; and
Complete the conduct of one-time PPE cleansing following the procedures in COA Circular.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Require the active Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances to settle and liquidate immediately, otherwise, consider withholding the payment of any money due pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,1997;
Issue demand letters to Accountable Officers who were separated, retired or transferred to another agency for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances, and ascertain the collectability of receivable and request for the write off on the dormant accounts following the guidelines in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Revisit and review the previous request for write off and submit the lacking documents as instructed by the ATL; and
Exert more effort in communicating with COA ICFAU of the pending Credit Notices by sending follow-up letter attaching the duly received transmittal letters of the previously submitted liquidation report for CIF.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require:
The City Accountant, City Budget Officer and CGSO to coordinate and review the accounts payable, the deliveries made and make necessary adjustments in the books for undelivered goods or services not rendered; and
The City Accountant to take up the accounts payable when goods are already delivered and services had been rendered in compliance with Section 19(b) of IPSAS 19.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to implement a cut-off procedure in the submission of claims to their Office prior to closing of books to ensure that all services rendered, and goods delivered as of the end of the year are recorded in the books either as expense or asset and its corresponding accounts payable in the correct accounting period.

5.11 We recommended, and Management agreed to require the City Accountant to properly review the transactions and ensure that payments for consultants, COS, and BAC Honorarium are properly recorded in the correct accounts consistent with IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Make representation to the Philhealth that the City Government will follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received by the City Government from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage;
Direct the Accounting Office to record revenue and receivable upon rendering the service supported by List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC, and avoid accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as TF; and
Permanently assign or hire personnel in the CHO task to prepare, submit and monitor all the claims submitted to PHIC, and regularly submit to the Accounting Office the claimable services rendered for timely recognition of revenue.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher and check to effect the transfer of the unexpended DRRMF balance of ₱210,150.13 to the TF books.

Management to direct and require the: Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile their records immediately and regularly to ensure the presentation of reliable FS; City Accountant and City Treasurer to prepare the CB and GL reconciliation statement on a monthly basis which shall be jointly signed by them; City Accountant to furnish the City Treasurer with the monthly bank reconciliation statements including those of the previous months and the latter to record in the CB the necessary adjustment/s; City Accountant to request copies of Credit Memoranda from the LBP Accounting Unit to enable the identification of the source agency corresponding to the deposited/downloaded amount.

1. Require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to: a. Monitor the submission and consolidation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report to be submitted to the Accounting Office for recording the fuel consumption in the books; b. Require drivers of the respective offices to prepare and submit the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report to the CGSO; c. Prepare the RIS for every issuance of supplies and materials to end users; d. Summarize the issuances per RIS in the RSMI at end of each month; e. Submit the RSMI together with the supporting RIS to the Accounting Office as basis for the recording of the expense and reduction of the corresponding inventory account; f. Reconstruct the RIS for items already issued to the end users and to prepare the corresponding RSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office. 2. Direct the Accounting Office to: a. Stop the practice of recording as direct expenses, inventory items procured, rather, recognize all procured inventories in the appropriate Inventory accounts; b. Ensure that in recording the issuance of inventory items, the relevant RSMI and RIS are prepared and submitted as supporting documents; c. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize the subject items procured in its proper inventory account which is Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution and submit to the auditor the journal entry voucher on the adjustment; d. Obtain the statement/ summary of withdrawals of fuel, oil and lubricants from the Supplier with details on the data and quantity of FOL withdrawn for reconciliation with the consolidated report of fuel consumption from the CGSO.

Require the Accounting Office to reverse the accounting entry recognizing liability for undelivered goods and services not rendered and provide copies to concerned Offices, as provided for by pertinent guidelines for its distribution; and Stop the practice of recognizing liability without the necessary condition, specifically the requirement for delivery of the items procured and/or performance of the service, as a precedent for recognizing such liabilities.

The City Accountant to: Review and analyse the journal entries on the proper classification of all disaster related transactions in conformity with the GAM for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2014-002 and thereafter, effect the necessary accounting adjustments; and Apprise the bookkeepers on the proper classification of transactions and usage of the Chart of Account.
Management to discontinue the practice of granting CAs to the Cashiers performing collecting functions, LOs and personnel who are not duly designated as SDOs in adherence to COA Circular No. 97-002.

To improve the business tax assessment process of the City, Management to: a. Adopt a policy requiring the business owner to submit their ITR to support the sworn statement of sales declaration to ensure that the declared income is more reliable as basis for assessment, in consonance to the BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020; b. Validate unrenewed business permit by assigning personnel to check if indeed the businesses are no longer in operation; c. Require the City Accountant to consolidate the Alpha List of Income Tax Payors of the City and that of the barangays in the City of Dapitan and furnish a copy to the City Treasurer; d. Require the personnel in charge in the assessment to verify or validate the declared income of the business owner to the Consolidated Alpha List of Income Tax Payors prepared by the City Accountant as reference in making business tax assessment; e. Codify the City Treasurer’s practices and processes to have a uniform approach in handling assessment and other forms of activity in the Office of the Treasurer towards its clients; and f. Require the regular inspection of the business establishment to check the accuracy of their declared income and its business classification and design a written report on the result of its inspection.

The City Assessor ensure the completion of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties and submit the same to the local SP for consideration and approval; and The SP review immediately upon receipt of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties as its basis in adopting an ordinance updating the general revision of real property assessment and classification as required by Section 219 of RA No. 7160 in order for the City to improve its revenue from RPT so that more services can be extended to the community.
Management to impose and/or collect the rates as mandated by the Revised Revenue Code of 2014, and if not implemented, obtain authority from the SP for the postponement/deferment of its imposition.

Management to: Observe the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of consulting services; Require the consultants and advisors to submit their credentials to determine and assess their capacities to undertake the work for which they were hired.

Management to: Continue the collection of garbage fee to augment the cost of transporting the wastes to the MRF and to cover maintenance cost of garbage truck/equipment; and Fast track the drafting of the proposed amendments to the City Ordinance.
Management to follow-up on the return of the mobilization cost paid, without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under existing laws.

1.7  We recommended that Management should require the continued reconciliation of the cashbook and SL balances of the CAO and CTO and to submit the same to COA on or before May 31, 2022 to ensure the completeness and accuracy of recorded transactions and balances of Cash in Bank accounts.
4.7  We recommended that the City Accountant should review properly all accounting entries made to avoid misclassification of accounts in order to convey a more accurate report.

6.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Internal Audit Services Department to review the processes and make recommendation to the Chief Executive to attain the objective of preparing and submitting the financial reports on time to the Office of the Auditor.

7.10  We recommended that Management direct the:
City Accountant:
1. To refrain from recording in the OMOE account, expenditures for which specific accounts have been provided in the RCALGU; and
2. To resort to realignments or supplemental budget in case the appropriations for the particular expenses are already exhausted.
b.  City Budget Officer to prepare and present the budget in accordance with the requirements of Section 317 (b)(3) of RA 7160 showing the detailed budgetary requirements containing the brief description of the programs and projects and the specific activities including the desired results together with the object of expenditures in order to ensure proper charging and to avoid subjective judgment in the recording of expenses using the account OMOE.

8.14  We recommended that Management  should require the:
a.  Proponent of the various programs and SDO that procurement be made through applicable procurement method to ensure that goods and services are acquired at the most advantageous prices and in a transparent process, and would allow the withholding of taxes due the NG;
b.  Payment of goods and services through check payable to the supplier or dealer except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check; and
c.  City Accountant not to process the grant of CAs for procurement of goods and services which should be procured through the prescribed procurement method.

9.6  We recommended to Management that:
a. The AIP shall only include specific PPAs for submission to the City Development Council (CDC) for approval; and
b.  For the CDC to approve only a resolution in which the AIP does not include a lump sum appropriation, in order to meet the requirement of Section 306 (b) of RA 7160.

10.6 We recommended to Management that the Certificate of Final Acceptance should be prepared and signed only after one year from the date of project completion in order to comply with the requirement of Section 62.2.2. of the Revised IRR of RA  9184.

12.9  We recommended that Management should require the City Accountant to:
Adjust the affected accounts and submit to COA copy of the JEV by May 31, 2022;
Review the accounting entries made by the bookkeepers to ensure that the appropriate account is used to record disbursements from the calamity fund and in conformity with COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
Submit the monthly RSMI together with the duly accomplished and signed RIS for the Food Supplies Inventory and Welfare Goods inventory that were recorded as expense as of December 31, 2021.

13.6  We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person and other responsible officials to submit to the Office of the Auditor the GAD PB and AR within the prescribed period to ensure prompt audit of GAD transactions.

The Accounting Office to review the composition of PPE and reclassify misclassified accounts, derecognize dismantled structures and PPE, the utilization of which is under the control by other offices, or its barangays. Specifically, we recommended that management observe the following in conducting the inventory and reconciliation thereof: The City Mayor is enjoined to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all PPEs of the City Government. Likewise, form several teams to assist the Inventory Committee in fulfilling its task; Prepare a written instruction on how to conduct the physical count, define its time table for completion and submission of report. In preparing the instruction, consider the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which allow a one-time cleansing of the official records on PPE; Submit a monthly report not later than 10 calendar days after end of the month a status and accomplishment report of the Inventory Committee to the Office of the Auditor; and Submit to the auditor the JEV on any adjustments made as a result of the inventory taking and reconciliation.

Management to require the contractor and/or project implementer, in cases when a project is implemented by administration to: a. Strictly adhere to the requirement of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and b. Immediately install the required billboards at the projects sites of the enumerated on-going projects and submit to the Auditor the picture taken of each billboard at project cite within 15 days from receipt hereof.
The City Accountant: Stop the setting up of obligations not yet due and demandable when there are no approved contracts.

Management to: Prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the items and to clear some space so that more valuable items could be properly kept in the area; and Reconstitute the Appraisal Committee and designate those who are of lesser work load than the present membership to facilitate prompt actions on disposal of unserviceable properties.

Management require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to: Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formation; Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

Management to require the City Assessor to coordinate with the City Engineer and CGSO to conduct an ocular verification of the above occupied lots and to compare the location with the city tax map to ascertain the ownership of the said lots and come up with a record of real properties identified after the ocular verification.
Management requires: c. the City Treasurer, City Accountant and CGSO to maintain and keep on file complete records of the City’s real-properties.

We reiterated our recommendation for Management to issue an Office Memorandum creating a team that will perform the physical inventory taking and to prepare the covering RPCPPE, to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records with the results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.

Management to: a. Coordination of the CGSO and the responsible offices that requisitioned the above inventories to assist in the retrieval of the source documents to support the eventual dropping from the books; b. Performance of physical inventory every time there is change in CGSO to set apart their accountabilities. Require the City Accountant and CGSO to prepare the SLs and stock cards of each Inventory account to facilitate reconciliation and identification of items for dropping from the books of account; and other related actions.

Management to: a. Implement the projects as scheduled so that the desired services and benefits could be delivered to its beneficiaries on a timely manner; and b. Employ a monitoring mechanism to assess the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
Management directs the Market Committee to strictly implement and impose penalty against erring Lessees for violating the rules in Section7A.09, Article A, Chapter 7 of the City Ordinance.

Management to: Enforce and implement the new increase rate of fees for rooms and stalls prescribed under the 2014 Revised Revenue Code (RRC) to raise more revenues and sustain maintenance cost of the public market; and Record properly all collections in the books and reflect its correct assessment.

Management direct the CGSO to submit the updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

The City Accountant:
a. Stop the setting up of obligations not yet due and demandable when there are no approved contracts; and
b. Adjust the payable account by ensuring that only approved contracts are recognized as payables.
observations.reason: Reiterated in paragraph 4 Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
Management require:
a. The City Assessor ensure the completion of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties and submit the same to the local SP for consideration and approval; and
b. The SP review immediately upon receipt of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties as its basis in adopting an ordinance updating the general revision of real property assessment and classification as required by Section 219 of RA No. 7160 in order for the City to improve its revenue from RPT so that more services can be extended to the community.
Management to impose and/or collect the rates as mandated by the Revised Revenue Code of 2014, and if not implemented, obtain authority from the SP for the postponement/deferment of its imposition.
observations.reason: The 2014 RRC needs to be revised to suit the prevailing situation.

observations.recommendation:
Management to:
a. Prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the items and to clear some space so that more valuable items could be properly kept in the area; and
b. Reconstitute the Appraisal Committee and designate those who are of lesser work load than the present membership to facilitate prompt actions on disposal of unserviceable properties.

Management to:
a. Observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of consulting services;
b. Consider tapping the services of its in-house personnel who are already capable, trained and have acquired in the work being assigned to the consultants and advisors; and
c. Require the consultants and advisors to submit reported their credentials to determine and assess their capacities to undertake the work for which they were hired.

Management require:
a. the City Assessor to coordinate with the City Engineer and CGSO to conduct an ocular verification of the above occupied lots and to compare the location with the city tax map to ascertain the ownership of the said lots and come up with a record of real properties identified after the ocular verification.
observations.reason: Ocular inspection is still on-going

observations.recommendation:
Management require:
a. The coordination of the taskforce with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) to verify whether said real properties were already transferred in the name of the LGU or otherwise; and
b. Initiate the titling of the parcels of the land purchased by the LGU to establish legal and true ownership of the real estate; and
c. Require the City Treasurer, City Accountant and CGSO to maintain and keep on file complete records of the City’s real properties.
observations.reason: Coordination and verification with LRA is still on-going. Source documents of most acquired lands are still being retrieved. While those lots with complete supporting documents are now in the titling stage. Records from CY 2018 to present are already being kept and updated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for Management to issue an Office Memorandum creating a team that will perform the physical inventory taking and to prepare the covering RPCPPE, to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records with the results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.
observations.reason: Preparation of the RPCPPE is still on-going

observations.recommendation:
Management to require the:
a. Coordination of the CGSO and the responsible offices that requisitioned the above inventories to assist in the retrieval of the source documents to support the eventual dropping from the books; and
b. Performance of physical inventory every time there is change in CGSO to set apart their accountabilities.
c. Require the City Accountant and CGSO to prepare the SLs and stock cards of each Inventory account to facilitate reconciliation and identification of items for dropping from the books of account;
observations.reason: Retrieval of the source documents still on-going. No change in CGSO management. Updating of the SLs and stock cards are on-going

observations.recommendation:
Management to:
a. Implement the projects as scheduled so that the desired services and benefits could be delivered to its beneficiaries on a timely manner; and
b. Employ a monitoring mechanism to assess the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.reason: Delay in identifying the site and authorization from the Secretary of DA and Bureau of BFAR

observations.recommendation:
Management to:
a. Continue the collection of garbage fee to augment the cost of transporting the wastes to the MRF and to cover maintenance cost of garbage truck/equipment; and
b. Fast track the drafting of the proposed amendments to the City Ordinance
observations.reason: Revision/amendment of the Ordinance was deferred due to the pandemic

observations.recommendation:
Management direct the Market Committee to:
a. Strictly implement and impose penalty against erring Lessees for violating the rules in Section7A.09, Article A, Chapter 7 of the City Ordinance; and
b. Conduct constant inspection and monitoring to keep abreast in the market operations.

Management to direct the Market Committee and the Treasury Department thru the Market Supervisor to:
a. Enforce and implement the new increase rate of fees for rooms and stalls prescribed under the 2014 Revised Revenue Code (RRC) to raise more revenues and sustain maintenance cost of the public market; and
b. Record properly all collections in the books and reflect its correct assessment.
observations.reason: Revision/ amendment of the 2014 RRC was deferred due to the pandemic. Updating of records is on-going

observations.recommendation: Management direct the CGSO to submit the updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary coverage for all assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund;
observations.reason: Reconciliation and revaluation of all properties is not yet finished.

observations.recommendation: Management to follow-up the return of the mobilization cost paid, without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under existing laws.
observations.reason: Management is still negotiating with the contractor

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that management direct and require the:
a. Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile their records immediately and
regularly to ensure the presentation of reliable FS;
b. City Accountant and City Treasurer to prepare the CB and GL reconciliation
statement on a monthly basis which shall be jointly signed by them;
c. City Accountant to furnish the City Treasurer with the monthly bank
reconciliation statements including those of the previous months and the latter
to record in the CB the necessary adjustment/s; and
d. City Accountant to request copies of Credit Memoranda from the Land Bank
of the Philippines (LBP) Accounting Unit to enable the identification of the
source agency corresponding to the deposited/downloaded amount.

2.15 We recommended that management:
Require the CGSO to:
a. Monitor the submission and consolidation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption
Report to be submitted to the Accounting Office for recording the fuel
consumption in the books;
b. Require drivers of the respective offices to prepare and submit the Monthly
Fuel Consumption Report to the CGSO;
c. Prepare the RIS for every issuance of supplies and materials to end users;
d. Summarize the issuances per RIS in the RSM at end of each month;
e. Submit the RSMI together with the supporting RIS to the Accounting Office
as basis for the recording of the expense and reduction of the corresponding
inventory account;
f. Reconstruct the RIS for items already issued to the end users and to prepare
the corresponding RSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office.
Direct the Accounting Office to:
a. Stop the practice of recording as direct expenses, inventory items procured,
rather, recognize all procured inventories in the appropriate Inventory
accounts;
b. Ensure that in recording the issuance of inventory items, the relevant RSMI
and RIS are prepared and submitted as supporting documents; and
c. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize the subject items
procured in its proper inventory account which is Other Supplies and Materials
for Distribution and submit to the auditor the journal entry voucher on the
adjustment.
47
d. Obtain the statement/summary of withdrawals of fuel, oil and lubricants from
the Supplier with details on the data and quantity of FOL withdrawn for
reconciliation with the consolidated report of fuel consumption from the CGSO

3.8 The Accounting Office should review the composition of PPE and reclassify
misclassified accounts, derecognize dismantled structures and PPE, the
utilization of which is under the control by other offices, or its barangays.
Specifically, we recommended that management observe the following in
conducting the inventory and reconciliation thereof:
a. The City Mayor is enjoined to create an inventory committee to conduct the
physical count of all PPEs of the City Government. Likewise, form several
teams to assist the Inventory Committee in fulfilling its task;
b. Prepare a written instruction on how to conduct the physical count, define its
time table for completion and submission of report. In preparing the
instruction, consider the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which allow
a one-time cleansing of the official records on PPE;
c. Submit a monthly report not later than 10 calendar days after end of the
month a status and accomplishment report of the Inventory Committee to the
Office of the Auditor; and
d. Submit to the auditor the journal entry voucher (JEV) on any adjustments
made as a result of the inventory taking and reconciliation.

4.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Accounting Office to reverse the accounting entry recognizing
liability for undelivered goods and services not rendered and provide copies to
concerned Offices, as provided for by pertinent guidelines for its distribution; and
b. Stop the practice of recognizing liability without the necessary condition,
specifically the requirement for delivery of the items procured and/or
performance of the service, as a precedent for recognizing such liabilities.

5.23 We recommended that the City Accountant:
a. Review and analyze the journal entries on the proper classification of all
disaster related transactions in conformity with the GAM for LGUs and COA
Circular No. 2014-002 and thereafter, effect the necessary accounting
adjustments; and
b. Apprise the bookkeepers on the proper classification of transactions and usage
of the Chart of Accounts

6.3 We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of granting CAs
to the Cashiers performing collecting functions, LOs and personnel who are not
duly designated as SDOs in adherence to COA Circular No. 97-002.

7.10 We recommended the following to improve the business tax assessment
process of the City:
a. Adopt a policy requiring the business owner to submit their ITR to support the
sworn statement of sales declaration to ensure that the declared income is more
reliable as basis for assessment, in consonance to the BLGF Memorandum
Circular No. 01-2020;
b. Validate unrenewed business permit by assigning personnel to check if indeed
the businesses are no longer in operation;
c. Require the City Accountant to consolidate the Alpha List of Income Tax Payors
of the City and that of the barangays in the City of Dapitan and furnish a copy to
the City Treasurer;
d. Require the personnel in charge in the assessment to verify or validate the
declared income of the business owner to the Consolidated Alpha List of Income
Tax Payors prepared by the City Accountant as reference in making business tax
assessment;
e. Codify the City Treasurer’s practices and processes to have a uniform approach
in handling assessment and other forms of activity in the Office of the Treasurer
towards its clients; and
f. Require the regular inspection of the business establishment to check the
accuracy of their declared income and its business classification and design a
written report on the result of its inspection.

8.4 We recommended that Management require the contractor and/or project
implementer, in cases when a project is implemented by administration to:
a. Strictly adhere to the requirement of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
b. Immediately install the required billboards at the projects’ sites of the
enumerated on-going projects and submit to the Auditor the picture taken of
each billboard at project cite within 15 days from receipt hereof.

9.9 We recommended that management designate a focal person for special
projects to monitor and ensure the completion of these projects.
The City Accountant prepare a JEV to adjust the A/R and Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable and to ensure the non-recurrence of similar inadvertences.

Management to:
a. Prioritize and expedite the implementation of BuB projects so that intended beneficiaries could benefit from the immediate and maximum use of the programs/activities;
b. Formulate projects that are attainable and available in the locality; and,
c. Advice the BAC and personnel in-charge of procurement to revisit the procurement process, identify points of delay and establish measures to address the flaw to avoid recurrence of delay in future similar program.

Management to:
a. Secure immediately the clearance/attestation of the NHC and make constant follow-ups in the approval of the plan for the improvement of the Dapitan City Plaza;
b. Comply immediately whatever the necessary papers/documents the NHC may require; and
c. Fast track the implementation of the project to avoid the reversion of the funds and beat the deadline for completing the project.

Management to:
a. Stop allocating appropriations for projects in lump-sum;
b. Require the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan containing the detailed information of the specific programs/projects to be funded from the 20 per cent EDF; and,
c. Review the availability of technical personnel, equipment and feasibility of implementing the programs/projects within realistic timelines.

Management to refrain from proposing projects through lump-sum appropriations and see to it that specific projects are already identified in the LDRRMF budget so that these can be implemented and completed within the year.

Management to require the concerned offices to facilitate the compliance with the above requirements and designate a focal person to take charge of securing the required ECC in order to fully operate the SLF.

Management to require the Market Supervisor to strictly require all stall/room owners to comply with the requirements of displaying their renewed Certificate of Award, DTI Certificate, Mayor’s Permit and Pictures with that of their helper/s conveniently framed in a conspicuously location in their respective establishment for monitoring purposes.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the GFPS Chairperson submit and furnish the Auditor copies Annual GAD Accomplishment Report together with the duly reviewed and endorsed Annual GPB within the prescribed period, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

Management:
a. Require the City Accountant to appropriately record above transactions to its proper account to avoid misstatement in the financial statements.
Management direct the City Treasurer to remit the 5 per cent of the income to the account of the DPWH Secretary under Special Account No. 154 thru the BTr;
Management to maintain accurate records of lease holders for proper accounting and assessment;
Management require the CGSO to keep all government motor vehicles within the premises of the NCGC.

We recommend that management require coordination by the CGSO with the City Accountant, City Budget Officer and City Treasurer to find a solution for the dropping of the GOL, with appropriate supporting documents/ appropriation.
observations.reason: Observation rephrased/reiterated in paragraph 2 Part II of CY 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:
1.13.1 Stop the setting up of obligations not yet due and demandable when there are no approved contracts; and
Adjust the payable account by ensuring that only approved contracts are recognized as payables.

Recommendation:
2.9 We recommended that the City Accountant prepare a JEV to adjust the Accounts Receivable and Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable and to ensure the non-recurrence of similar inadvertences.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
3.8.1 Prioritize and expedite the implementation of BuB projects so that intended beneficiaries could benefit from the immediate and maximum use of the programs/activities;
Formulate projects that are attainable and available in the locality; and,
Advice the BAC and personnel in-charge of procurement to revisit the procurement process, identify points of delay and establish measures to address the flaw to avoid recurrence of delay in future similar program.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management:
4.10.1 Secure immediately the clearance/attestation of the NHC and make constant follow-ups in the approval of the plan for the improvement of the Dapitan City Plaza;
Comply immediately whatever the necessary papers/documents the NHC may require; and
Fast track the implementation of the project to avoid the reversion of the funds and beat the deadline for completing the project.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Stop allocating appropriations for projects in lump-sum;
5.10.2 Require the preparation of the AIP containing the detailed information of the specific programs/projects to be funded from the 20 per cent EDF; and,
5.10.3 Review the availability of technical personnel, equipment and feasibility of implementing the programs/projects  within realistic timelines.

Recommendation:
6.13 We recommended that management refrain from proposing projects through lump-sum appropriations and see to it that specific projects are already identified in the LDRRMF budget so that these can be implemented and completed within the year.

Recommendation:
7.8 We recommended that Management require the concerned offices to facilitate the compliance with the above requirements and designate a focal person to take charge of securing the required ECC in order to fully operate the SLF.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
8.6.1 The City Assessor ensure the completion of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties and submit the same to the local SP for consideration and approval; and
8.6.2 The SP review immediately upon receipt of the Schedule of Market Values, Assessment Levels and Classification of Real Properties as its basis in adopting an ordinance updating the general revision of real property assessment and classification as required by Section 219 of RA No. 7160 in order for the City to improve its revenue from RPT so that more services can be extended to the community.

Recommendation
9.13 We recommended that Management impose/collect the rates as mandated by the Revised Revenue Code of 2014, and if not implemented, obtain authority from the SP for the postponement/deferment of its imposition.

Recommendation:
10.4 We recommended that Management require the Market Supervisor to strictly require all stall/room owners to comply with the requirements of displaying their renewed Certificate of Award, DTI Certificate, Mayor’s Permit and Pictures with that of their helper/s conveniently framed in a conspicuously location in their respective establishment for monitoring purposes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management:
11.5.1 Prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the items and to clear some space so that more valuable items could be properly kept in the area; and,
Reconstitute the Appraisal Committee and designate those who are of lesser work load than the present membership to facilitate prompt actions on disposal of unserviceable properties.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
12.10.1 Observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of consulting services;
12.10.2 Consider tapping the services of its in-house personnel who are already capable, trained and have acquired in the work being assigned to the consultants and advisors; and
12.10.3 Require the consultants and advisors to submit their credentials to determine and assess their capacities to undertake the work for which they were hired.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the GFPS Chairperson submit and furnish the Auditor copies Annual GAD Accomplishment Report together with the duly reviewed and endorsed Annual GPB within the prescribed period, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that Management require:

a. the City Accountant to record the six acquisitions; and

b. the City Assessor to coordinate with the City Engineer and City General Services Officer to conduct an ocular verification of the above occupied lots and to compare the location with the city tax map to ascertain the ownership of the said lots and come up with a record of real properties identified after the ocular verification.

We reiterated our recommendation for Management to issue an Office Memorandum creating a team that will perform the physical inventory taking and to prepare the covering Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records with the results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.

We recommended that Management to require the:

a. Coordination of the CGSO and the responsible offices that requisitioned the above inventories to assist in the retrieval of the source documents to support the eventual dropping from the books; and

b. Performance of physical inventory every time there is change in CGSO to set apart their accountabilities.

We recommended for Management to:

Implement the projects as scheduled so that the desired services and benefits could be delivered to its beneficiaries on a timely manner; and

Employ a monitoring mechanism to assess the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
We recommended that Management make use of the existing MOA to expedite the procurement of relief goods to address the emergency sought on time.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documents to attest on the relevance and competence of the document as evidence in support to the claim;  

Submit the certified list of beneficiaries/ recipients of relief medicines and or other equivalent document to serve as basis for confirmation by the Audit Team; and

Require the City Accountant to appropriately record above transactions to its proper account to avoid misstatement in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

Continue the collection of garbage fee to augment the cost of transporting the wastes to the MRF and to cover maintenance cost of garbage truck/equipment; and

Fast track the drafting of the proposed amendments to the City Ordinance

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management: 

Fully utilize the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) - payroll facility in paying personnel remunerations and wages of job orders and consultants; or if not applicable; and

Increase the bond of the AO.

We recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer to:

Remit the 5 per cent of the income to the account of the DPWH Secretary under Special Account No. 154 thru the BTr; 

Immediately notify the DPWH Secretary thru the NBCDO that its 5 per cent share has already been remitted; and

c. Faithfully observe and strictly comply with the remittances of the share of DPWH from the building permit fee collections pursuant to Section 210 of PD 1096.

We recommended that Management direct the Market Committee to:

Strictly implement and impose penalty against erring Lessees for violating the rules in Section7A.09, Article A, Chapter 7 of the City Ordinance; and

Conduct constant inspection and monitoring to keep abreast in the market operations.

We recommended that Management direct the Market Committee and the Treasury Department thru the Market Supervisor to:

Enforce and implement the new increase rate of fees for rooms and stalls prescribed under the 2014 Revised Revenue Code (RRC) to raise more revenues and sustain maintenance cost of the public market; and

Record properly all collections in the books and reflect its correct assessment.

We recommended that Management to:

Direct the Market Committee to renew all expired Contract of Lease to deserving/qualified Lessees and/or issue new/reconstructed ones of those Lessees with lost copies/documents on file;

Secure/obtain file copies of these duly notarized renewed and reconstructed Lease Contracts; and

See to it that appropriate fees/charges are collected and properly recorded, for income purposes.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to refrain from processing claims without complete supporting documents in accordance with established laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management to allocate no less than 5 per cent of its total annual appropriations for GAD, in compliance with Joint Circular No. 2012-01. This is to adopt and ensure optimum compliance following gender mainstreaming to promote women’s rights, gender issues are effectively addressed, seeing to it that the PPAs are aligned to its mandate – the Philippine Plan for Gender and Development (PPGD) for 1995-2025 and other laws on GAD; to cover planning, implementation, monitoring and evaluation in the utilization of GAD funds for which they have been appropriated.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson to submit and furnish the Auditor copies of the duly reviewed and endorsed GPB and Accomplishment Report yearly, and within the prescribed period, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
Coordination by the CGSO and the responsible offices that requisitioned the  inventories to assist in the retrieval of the source documents to support the eventual dropping of Inventories Held for Consumption from the books; and
Performance of physical inventory every time there is a change in CGS Officer to set apart their accountabilities.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management issue an Office Memorandum creating a team that will conduct physical inventory taking and to prepare the covering Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records with the results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management to require the:
City Accountant to record the six acquisitions of Land and the seven lots occupied by the City in the  SLs; and
City Assessor to coordinate with the City Engineer and CGSO on conducting ocular verification of the seven occupied lots, to compare the location with the city tax map to ascertain the ownership thereof, come up with a record of real properties identified after the ocular verification, and to take these up in the accounting records.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
City Accountant to reclassify the 28 real estate to its appropriate account classification based on the purpose and actual usage during acquisition;
Creation of a taskforce that will focus in the production/retrieval of necessary documents for the eventual titling of lands in the name of the LGU; and
Coordination of the taskforce with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) to verify whether said real properties were already transferred in the name of the LGU or otherwise.

We recommended that Management require the:

The City Assessor to prepare and submit on or before the 31st day of December of each year, an assessment roll, to the City Treasurer to enable the latter to prepare and submit the RPT Account Register to the City Accountant for the setting up of the Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Fund Tax Income at beginning of each year.
observations.managementAction:
The City Assessor, Treasurer and Accountant coordinated with each other and comply with an assessment roll for CY 2018.

The OIC City Treasurer prepared and submitted to the City Accountant the CY 2018 certified list of taxpayers containing the amount due and collectible for the year.

The City Accountant set up the RPTR using the amounts contained in the certified list submitted by the OIC City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

City Accountant and CGSO to prepare the SLs and stock cards of each Inventory account to facilitate reconciliation and identification of items for dropping from the books of account; and

Conduct of physical inventory:

b.1  Every time there is change in the CGS Officer to set apart their accountabilities; and

b.2 To verify the recorded balances for reconciliation with the amount in the Balance Sheet.
observations.managementAction:
Physical inventory taking was conducted when there is change in CGSO to set apart their accountabilities.

Stock Cards and SLs already established but there are recorded lump amount that needs to be traced to the source document.

The CGSO and City Accountant to coordinated with each other and was able to find/retrieve some of the source documents supporting the dropping of the GOL account.
observations.reason: During the year there was a change of CGSO, but only records were turned over. No physical taking of Inventories was conducted.

observations.recommendation:
Initiate the titling of the parcels of the land purchased by the LGU to establish legal and true ownership of the real estate; 
Require the SP to appropriate funds needed to secure Transfer Certificate of Title (TCTs) for the LGU real estate.

Require the City Treasurer, City Accountant and CGSO to maintain and keep on file complete records of the City’s real properties; and

Require the continued coordination of said Offices to locate the source documents pertaining to the acquisition of real estate for the eventual titling in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Already created the Task Force to look into the titling of all land acquired by the City Government.

As of December 31, 2018 only one (1) lot was titled in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason:
Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 4 page 49 of this AAR.

During the year, the number of untitled lots totalled 10 parcels.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation for Management to issue an order for the different inventory committees to complete the physical inventory taking and prepare the covering RPCPPE, to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records with the results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.
observations.managementAction: Only records of unserviceable property were submitted in 2018
observations.reason: Change of CGSO and non-conduct of physical inventory of PPE during the year

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of drawing  CAs to pay for goods and services;

Faithfully adhere with established law, rules and regulation on Procurement and CA; and

Henceforth to withhold the taxes due from suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the granting of CAs to pay for goods and services and adhere with requirement to withhold taxes due from suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the: 

CGSO to submit the updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund;

City Budget Officer to appropriate an amount to cover the insurance of the insurable PPEs; and

City Accountant and OIC City Treasurer to ensure that the insurable properties of the City are duly insured with the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: The City Budget Office already appropriated an amount to cover the insurance of the insurable PPEs, City Accountant’s Office and OIC City Treasurer saw to it that the insurable properties of the City were duly insured with the GSIS Insurance Fund.
observations.reason: Change of CGSO and non-conduct of physical inventory of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government include in their CY 2018 budget an appropriation for the establishment of an archival system and construction of a building to house its records, and to include these projects in the AIP for said year.
observations.managementAction:
Already identified the area where the archive building will be constructed.

Appropriation for the archive building is already included in the CY 2019 budget, however, the plan and design of the building is not yet finished.
observations.reason: Not included in the CY 2018 budget but already identified the area where the archive building shall be constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the contractor, Stars and Stripes Construction, to pay for liquidated damages (LD) equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay in completing the projects as per Annex E of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were collected from the contractor

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Re-implement the dredging project to protect its residents against disasters, calamities and complex emergencies in accord with Section 2(a) of RA 10121; and 

To follow-up on the return of the mobilization cost paid, without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under existing laws.
observations.managementAction:
Project was re-implemented sourced from the balance of the contract amount.

Management made constant follow-up, written and verbal, for the return of the mobilization paid to the contractor

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Re-implement the dredging project to protect its residents against disasters, calamities and complex emergencies in accord with Section 2(a) of RA 10121; and 

To follow-up on the return of the mobilization cost paid, without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under existing laws.
observations.managementAction: Management made constant follow-up, written and verbal, for the return of the mobilization paid to the contractor
observations.reason: During the year, the owner of Madaja Construction died and the request for the cancellation of the Performance Bond with the Insuring company is still under legal action.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for Management to immediately return the balances of the PDAF and DAP completed projects to the BTr.
observations.managementAction: On October 9, 2018 the amount of  P12,587,851.69 was remitted to the BTr  representing the balances of PDAF and DAP funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Fully utilize of the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) Payroll Facility in paying personnel remuneration in Advances for Payroll account of the General Fund (GF);
observations.managementAction:
Full utilization of the ATM – payroll facility in paying personnel remunerations.

Refund of the unliquidated CAs of the AOs and employees are settled through payroll deduction.

No CAs were granted to employees with unliquidated CAs
observations.reason: JOs hired in intermitted contract are still paid from the CA drawn by an SDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant account for the difference in the accounts, trace and locate the relevant documents, to serve as basis in adjusting the identified reconciling item(s) in the respective reciprocal account for accurate and fair presentation of the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the FS.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of the accounts are still on-going.

For those accounts covering the period CY 2009 and prior, the City Accountant coordinated with the Office of the Audit Team Leader to lend them the FS of these years.
observations.reason: The Office of the City Accountant is not yet finished in reconciling the prior year’s balances of the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to direct the Hotel Manager to:

Continue the thorough reconciliation of the receivable account to establish the accuracy of the said account; and

Exert effort for the collection of the remaining outstanding Accounts Receivable to finance future cost of improvement, repair and/or expansion and other related operating expenditure of the hotel.
observations.managementAction: The Hotel Manager sent confirmation letters to the concerned hotel guests appearing in the Accounts Receivable ledger. Some replied but most of the letters were returned to sender due to incomplete/unknown address. These letters accounted for the recorded balance as of December 31, 2018
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the account is still on going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to keep all government motor vehicles within the premises of the New City Government Center.
observations.managementAction: A garage will be constructed to house all motor vehicles of the LGU.
observations.reason:
The garage to shelter all the motor vehicle of the LGU is yet to be constructed.

Appropriation for the garage is included in the CY 2019 Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the City Accountant to:

Set up the Real Property Tax Receivable (RPTR)/SETR at the beginning of each year based on the duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year furnished by the City Treasurer. 

Support the amounts reflected in the Trial Balance with list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.
observations.reason:
Reiterated under Audit Observation (AO) No. 1 pages 41 – 43  of this AAR

The City Treasurer failed to provide the Accounting Office with the certified list of taxpayers due to manpower shortage

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Initiate the titling of the parcels of the land purchased by the LGU to establish legal and true ownership of the real estate; 

Require the SP to appropriate funds needed to secure Transfer Certificate of Title (TCTs) for the LGU real estate.

Require the City Treasurer, City Accountant and City General Services Office (CGSO) to maintain and keep on file complete records of real properties; and

Require the continued coordination of said Offices to locate the source documents pertaining to the acquisition of real estate for eventual titling in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason:
Management is still locating the original copy of the title 

Non coordination of City Treasurer, City Accountant and CGSO due to personal conflict

No details provided in the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) upon acquisition/ payment of the real property(ies)

observations.recommendation: Find ways to put into use the RCM to generate additional income.
observations.reason: The MRF where the RCM is to be placed is still under construction and installation of electrical connection is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for management to return/ immediately refund the unused/ unutilized balances from PDAF and DAP to the BTr.
observations.reason:
Reiterated under AO No. 9 pages 56 - 57 of this AAR

Management is contemplating to request authority from the source agency to utilize the unexpended/ unutilized amount for other projects of the City.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

        We recommended that Management require the:


        a. The City Assessor to prepare and submit on or  before  the  31st
           day of December of each year, an assessment roll,  to  the  City
           Treasurer to enable the latter to prepare  and  submit  the  RPT
           Account Register to the City Accountant for the  setting  up  of
           the RPTR/SETR and DRPTI/DSEFTI at beginning of each year.


        b. OIC - City Treasurer and the City Assessor  to  coordinate  with
           each other in order to come up with the RPT Account Register;



        c. City Treasurer to submit the certified list of RPTR at beginning
           of the year;



        d. City Accountant to:


           d.1 Set up the RPTR/SETR and the corresponding  deferred  income
               at beginning of every year; and

           d.2  Account for collections corresponding  to  the  year  being
               paid to arrive  at  the  correct  receivable/deferred  income
               balances.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that Management:


        a. Require the City Accountant and CGSO  to  prepare  the  SLs  and
           stock  cards   of   each   Inventory   account   to   facilitate
           reconciliation and identification of items for dropping from the
           books of account; and


        b. Require the conduct of physical inventory every  time  there  is
           change in the CGS Officer to set apart their accountabilities.

Recommendation:


        We reiterated our recommendation that Management:


        a. Initiate the titling of the parcels of the land purchased by the
           LGU to establish legal and true ownership of the real estate;



        b. Require the SP to appropriate funds needed  to  secure  Transfer
           Certificate of Title (TCTs) for the LGU real estate.



        c. Require the City Treasurer, City Accountant and CGSO to maintain
           and keep on file complete records of the City’s real properties;
           and




        d. Require the continued coordination of said Offices to locate the
           source documents pertaining to the acquisition  of  real  estate
           for the eventual titling in the name of the LGU.

We reiterate our recommendation for Management to issue an order for
  the different inventory committees to  complete  the  physical  inventory
  taking and the preparation of the covering Report on the  Physical  Count
  of PPE, to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting records  with  the
  results of the physical count, as well as the CGSO records.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that Management:


        a. Stop the practice of drawing  CAs to pay for goods and services;



        b. Faithfully adhere with established law, rules and regulation  on
           Procurement and CA; and

        c. Henceforth to withhold the taxes due from suppliers.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that Management direct the:


        a. CGSO to submit the updated list of insurable properties with the
           GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets
           can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund;


        b. City Budget Officer  to  appropriate  an  amount  to  cover  the
           insurance of the insurable PPEs; and

        c. City Accountant and  OIC  City  Treasurer  to  ensure  that  the
           insurable properties of the City are duly insured with the  GSIS
           every year.

Recommendation:

        We recommended that the City Government include in  their  CY  2018
   budget an appropriation for the establishment of an archival  system  and
   construction of a building to house its records,  and  to  include  these
   projects in the AIP for said year.

We recommended that Management:

Fully utilize of the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) Payroll Facility in paying personnel remuneration in Advances for Payroll account of the General Fund (GF);

Desist from granting CAs if the previous advances are not liquidated.

Grant CAs based on the allowable travelling expenses authorized under Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

Require liquidation of CAs to SDOs and AOs within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001

Require the refund of undisbursed funds

Require the City Accountant to:

Furnish the Department Heads and Division Chiefs a list of long-outstanding unliquidated CAs of employees under their charge, including SDOs;

Abide by the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, including the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to render or settle unliquidated accounts within the prescribed period.

Institute appropriate legal sanction against employees concerned by withholding or suspension of their salaries under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128, PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Effective CY 2017, Management already required the Consultants and Job Order (JO) personnel to open an account with LBP – Dapitan Branch for the downloading of their salaries and wages.
observations.reason: Job Order employees of the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) were still paid through CA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant account for the difference in the accounts, trace and locate the relevant documents, to serve as basis in adjusting the identified reconciling item(s) in the respective reciprocal account for accurate and fair presentation of the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the said accounts still on going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Fast track the acquisition of land where the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) will be constructed. 

Keep the machine in a covered storage place to protect it against all weather condition and prevent pilferage of parts; and

Find ways to put into use the RCM to generate additional income.

We recommended that Management require the:

BuB Focal Person to prepare and submit the required reports as mandated under the DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7 for smooth release of funds intended for the implementation of BUB projects.

Chief of the City Planning and Development Office  to specify the BuB projects to be implemented on the given year for easy identification, instead of presenting in a lump-sum amount in the Annual Investment Plan.

Proponents of proposed BuB projects to conduct thorough planning in coming up with project proposals and consultations with residents to be affected by the implementation of the project to avoid delay.

LGU

Through the BuB Focal Person and the City Engineering Office (CEO), to submit progress reports on the project implementation to address immediately problems affecting the BuB operation;

To refrain from issuing notice of award to contractors/suppliers in the absence of receipt of funds from concerned National Government Agencies.

e.	BAC to invite at least three Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) signatories on bidding of BuB projects and have them affix their signatures on the bidding documents.

f. LPRAT to conduct regular meetings and monitoring of BuB projects.

We recommended that Management declare the contractor in default for failure to abide its obligations in the contract and take the following actions:

a. Require the contractor Madaja Builders and Construction Supply Corporation to refund to the LGU the amount of P2,147,712.00 representing claim of 15% mobilization amount;

b. Initiate proceedings/ collection on the performance security against Liberty Insurance Corporation for the non-performance of the obligation which resulted in the failure of the contractor to implement the dredging project as provided in Section 39.3 of the Revived IRR of RA 9184.
	
c. Prohibit/bar/blacklist the subject contractor to enter into any future contracts with the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management rescinded the contract and demanded from the contractor the refund of the 15% mobilization.
observations.reason: The contractor is amenable to refund the amount provided that they be allowed to deduct the expenses it incurred in mobilizing their equipment and manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable submit the required documentation and explanations/justifications listed in the notices of suspension (NS), to avoid unnecessary complications arising from suspensions lapsing into disallowances.
observations.reason: Most of the persons liable per NS are no longer connected / in the service of the City Government.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to direct the Hotel Manager to:

Continue the thorough reconciliation of the receivable account to establish the accuracy of the said account; and

Exert effort for the collection of the remaining outstanding Accounts Receivable to finance future cost of improvement, repair and/or expansion and other related operating expenditure of the hotel.
observations.managementAction: Management sent confirmation letters
observations.reason: Most confirmation letters sent were: a.) not responded to and b.) returned to sender because the addressee could not be located

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to keep all government motor vehicles within the premises of the New City Government Center (NGC).
observations.reason: There are still MVs not parked within the premises of the NGC

observations.recommendation: Require coordination by the CGSO with the City Accountant, City Budget Officer and City Treasurer to find a solution for the dropping of the GOL, with appropriate supporting documents/ appropriation.
observations.reason: Reiterated under AO No. 2 pages 43 - 45 of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous    recommendation for Management to issue an order for the different inventory committees to continue the conduct of inventory-taking and to complete the preparation of the inventory report within ten days from completion thereof.
observations.reason: Physical Inventory taking is still going on

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Officer conduct physical inventory and verify the recorded balances for reconciliation with the amount presented in the Balance Sheet.
observations.reason: Reiterated under AO No. 2 pages 43 - 45 of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the personnel in charge of the keeping of SLs, preparation of bank reconciliation report and financial statements to do the following:

Regularly coordinate with each other and reconcile cash in bank records; and

Locate and identify documents pertaining to discrepancies between book and bank balances so that adjustment can be effected immediately, and thus guaranty the accuracy and reliability of the cash account as presented in the Statement of Financial Position.

Recommendation:

      We recommended and management agreed to comply with COA  Circular  No.
   97-002 on the grant, utilization and  liquidation  of  cash  advance  and
   Section 1.2 of COA Circular No.  2012-001  on  its  liquidation,  and  to
   strictly adhere with Section 4 of EO 298 for travel cash advances.


   Management Comment:

      Management   is   in   agreement   with   the    recommendation.    To
   reduce/eliminate the unliquidated cash advances, the  following  are  the
   course of action to be undertaken:


     a. No cash advance shall be granted/allowed to personnel of  the  City
        Government with outstanding cash advance;


     b. Demand letters will be sent to the  Accountable  Officers  for  the
        immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advance(s); and


     c. If not complied, the outstanding cash advance will be deducted from
        the remuneration of the Accountable Officer.


   d. Selective utilization of the Automated Teller  Machine  (ATM)  Payroll
      Facility in paying personnel remuneration resulted in the accumulation
      of P3,685,538.91 in the Advances for Payroll  account  of  the  GF  as
      shown below:


   [pic]
   [pic]


   Recommendation:

        We reiterate our previous year’s recommendations that management:


        a. Make use of the  ATM-Payroll  facility  to  reduce  unliquidated
           payroll cash advance as a preventive  internal  control  measure
           over cash to  discourage  attempts  of  misappropriation  and/or
           malversation of public funds in the future;


        b. Require the accountable officers  to  refund  their  undisbursed
           funds; and

        c.  Strictly  enforce  established  laws,  rules   and   regulation
           governing cash advances.

   Management Comment:


      Effective CY 2017, management already required the Consultants and Job
   Orders (JO) personnel to open an account with LBP –  Dapitan  Branch  for
   the downloading of their salaries.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the City Accountant account for the difference  in
   the accounts, trace and locate the relevant documents, to serve as  basis
   in  adjusting  the  identified  reconciling  item(s)  in  the  respective
   reciprocal account for accurate and fair presentation of the balances  of
   the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the financial
   statements.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management require the City Accountant to:


     1. Set up the RPTR/SETR at the beginning of each  year  based  on  the
        duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
        for the year furnished by the City Treasurer.




     2. Support the amounts reflected in the Trial  Balance  with  list  of
        taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management:


     1. Initiate the titling of the parcels of the land  purchased  by  the
        LGU to establish legal and true ownership of the real estate;




     2. Require the Sangguniang Panglungsod to appropriate funds needed to
        secure Transfer Certificate of Title (TCTs) for the LGU real
        estate.




     3. Require the City Treasurer and City GSO to  maintain  and  keep  on
        file complete records of real properties;



     4. The City Accountant to set  up  and  maintain  complete  accounting
        records of real properties; and


     5. The continued coordination of said Offices  to  locate  the  source
        documents pertaining to the acquisition  of  real  estate  for  the
        eventual titling in the name of the LGU.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management:


     1.  Fast  track  the  acquisition  of  land  where  the  MRF  will  be
        constructed.





     2. Keep the machine in a covered storage place to protect  it  against
        all weather condition and prevent pilferage of parts; and



     3. Find ways to put into use the RCM to generate additional income.

Recommendation:

      We reiterated our recommendation for management to return/ immediately
   refund the unused/unutilized balances from PDAF and DAP to the BTr.


   Management Comment:

      The City Accountant assured the Audit Team that as  soon  as  the  on-
   going Barangay Impact Projects (BIPs) funded by  DAP  are  completed  and
   reconciliation of PDAF funds is finished, balances of said funds will  be
   remitted/returned to the BTr accordingly.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management require the:

        a. BuB Focal Person to prepare and submit the required  reports  as
           mandated under the DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No.  7  for  a  smooth
           release  of  funds  intended  for  the  implementation  of   BUB
           projects.


        b. Chief of the City Planning  and  Development  Office  (CPDO)  to
           specify the BuB projects to be implemented on the given year for
           easy identification, instead of presenting in a lump-sum  amount
           in the AIP.


        c. Proponents of proposed BuB projects to conduct thorough planning
           in coming up  with  project  proposals  and  consultations  with
           residents to be affected by the implementation of the project to
           avoid delay.


        d. LGU


              1. Through the  BuB  Focal  Person  and  the  CEO,  to  submit
                 progress reports on the project implementation  to  address
                 immediately problems affecting the BuB operation;


              2.   To   refrain   from   issuing   notice   of   award    to
                 contractors/suppliers in the absence of  receipt  of  funds
                 from concerned NGAs.


        e. BAC to invite at least three LPRAT signatories on bidding of BuB
           projects and have them affix their  signatures  on  the  bidding
           documents.


        f.  LPRAT  to  conduct  regular  meetings  and  monitoring  of  BuB
           projects.

   Management Comment:


      The BuB Focal Person thru the In-charge of Office, CPDO submitted  the
   BuB budgeting documents and status reports last January 24, 2017.

   Auditor’s Rejoinder:

      This constitutes  only  partial  compliance  of  the  recommendations.
   Hence, we request the implementation of the remaining recommendations  to
   comply with DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7 and RIRR of RA 9184.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management declare the contractor in  default  for
   failure to abide its obligations in the contract and take  the  following
   actions:


      a. Require the contractor  Madaja  Builders  and  Construction  Supply
        Corporation to  refund  to  the  LGU  the  amount  of  P2,147,712.00
        representing claim of 15% mobilization amount;


      b. Initiate proceedings/collection on the performance security against
        Liberty Insurance Corporation for the non-performance of  obligation
        which resulted in the failure of the  contractor  to  implement  the
        dredging project as provided in Section 39.3 of the Revived  IRR  of
        R.A. 9184.


      c. Prohibit/bar/blacklist the subject contractor  to  enter  into  any
        future contracts with the LGU.


   Management Comment:


      Management informed  the  Audit  Team  that  they  have  already  sent
   communication  to  the  contractor  requiring  the   submission   of   an
   explanation for the non-performance of  his  obligation  and  appropriate
   action shall be taken upon receipt of the response from the contractor.


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


      Management  must  pursue  actions  against  the  contractor  for   not
   implementing the project, depriving the residents of  the  benefits  from
   it.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management exercise prudence in their disbursement
   of public funds by adhering to established law, rules and regulations  to
   prevent incurrence of irregular expenditures.


   Management Comment:


      Management has always been in support and granted assistance to  every
   program of the City which has become part of  the  annual  activities  to
   lure participation among all Dapitanons.  Still,  management  gave  their
   assurance to abide to established rules and regulations in  dealing  with
   government transactions


   Auditor’s Rejoinder:


      There  is  no  legal  basis  for  the  grant  of  honoraria  to  media
   personalities.

Direct the personnel in charge of the keeping of subsidiary ledgers, preparation of bank reconciliation report and financial statements to do the following:

Regularly coordinate with each other and reconcile cash in bank records; and

Locate and identify documents pertaining to discrepancies between book and bank balances so that adjustment can be effected immediately, and thus guaranty the accuracy and reliability of the cash account as presented in the Statement of Financial Position (SFP).

Continue the thorough reconciliation of the receivable account to establish the accuracy of the said account; and

Exert effort for the collection of the remaining outstanding accounts receivable to finance future cost of improvement, repair and/or expansion and other related operating expenditure of the hotel.

Instruct Accountable Officers (AOs) to refrain from passing for payment altered receipts which may constitute falsification of public documents; and
